hypothesis: If fragment-based generation of linear polyamines is coupled with DFT-refined force fields, a two-stage MD/GCMC simulation workflow, and Bayesian optimization using polymer-specific pretrained embeddings, then higher low-pressure (0.4 mbar, 298 K) CO2-adsorbing candidates will be identified within a small simulation budget (≤30 evaluations) even from low-performing initializations (γ=0.3–0.5) because the surrogate-guided search captures structure–adsorption relationships more efficiently than uninformed sampling.
